what_it_does: This file is a terrain ledger entry that catalogues an external data directory called corpora, describing its contents, its role as the raw text source for a session-recall vector index, and its observed freshness state.
observations: Status is 'unconfirmed' despite being referenced by other entries
Relation is 'external' and coverage shows files_read: 0 out of 1863 total
Size is 93M across two subfolders: claude-platform-docs (811 .md) and claude-sessions (1051 .md + 1 .json)
Indexed-by [[cache-company]] via a vault pointing at claude-sessions
Derived-from [[company-systemd]] because a Company exporter writes markdown into claude-sessions
last_active is 2026-06-12, described as stale relative to an intended 20-minute export cadence
coverage.last_read is 2026-06-26, which is later than last_active
No git remote and secrets: false
The entry explicitly states no file bodies were read
standouts: Corpora is the source text that the session-recall vector index embeds
Only the transcript half (claude-sessions) is registered as a vault in cache-company
The data is flagged as stale because the exporter timer is inactive
The entry documents provenance without ingesting the actual files
conventions: Uses terrain-entry frontmatter with type, name, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, coverage, git_remote, indexed-by, derived-from, relates-to, and tags
Uses descriptive register for an observed/catalogued item
Uses [[...]] cross-references to link related ledger entries
Includes evidence-annotated observations and explicit read-depth disclaimers
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' even though it is already wired into cache-company
coverage.files_read is 0 while files_total is 1863, so the entry is based on structure rather than content
last_active is stale relative to the intended export cadence
coverage.last_read (2026-06-26) is later than last_active (2026-06-12), which is inconsistent without explanation
No git remote and no active refresh path noted
notes: A catalogue entry for the raw 'Claude Code Atlas' corpus that feeds the recall index, with a clear note that the data has not been refreshed recently.
questions: Why is coverage.last_read 2026-06-26 when last_active is only 2026-06-12?
What exactly distinguishes claude-platform-docs from claude-sessions in content and update mechanism?
Why is the Company exporter timer inactive?
Is corpora intended to be read directly, or only consumed through cache-company?
What embedding model or lens runs on port :8007?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as a shared source library and the source text for the session-recall index; the file matches that purpose by cataloguing the directory, its structure, and its downstream use, while also noting it is currently stale.
apparent_themes: Separation of raw text from indexed vector store
intention_signals: The entry seems to be trying to map the boundary between raw transcript/documentation data and the recall index, making the data flow and its current staleness visible without requiring the files to be read.
novelty: It treats scraped platform documentation and exported session transcripts as a single 'Atlas' corpus, and explicitly records that the export loop is inactive and that no file bodies were inspected.
contribution: A concise, evidence-annotated record of the corpora directory's role, structure, freshness, and upstream/downstream links, which lets a fused system understand the memory pipeline without reading 1863 files.
summary_for_embedding: A terrain ledger entry describing /home/tim/corpora, the 93M 'Claude Code Atlas' corpus of platform docs and session transcripts that feeds the session-recall vector index via cache-company, currently stale since 2026-06-12.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue the external corpora data source, its relationship to the recall index, and its observed freshness state.
